title: Young rangers explore history at Graaff-Reinet Museum
bodyText: [{"type":"paragraph","text":"GRAAFF-REINET NEWS - A group of 12 enthusiastic SANParks Young Rangers from the Karoo National Park in Beaufort West enjoyed a memorable and educational visit to the Graaff-Reinet Museum on Tuesday 7 October - an experience many described as a dream come true.","html":"GRAAFF-REINET NEWS - A group of 12 enthusiastic SANParks Young Rangers from the Karoo National Park in Beaufort West enjoyed a memorable and educational visit to the Graaff-Reinet Museum on Tuesday 7 October - an experience many described as a dream come true."},{"type":"blockquote","text":"The Young Rangers, also known as Junior Honorary Rangers (JHRs), form part of a national programme run by SANParks Honorary Rangers that encourages youth aged 12 to 18 to become involved with conservation and environmental awareness."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"Through interactive lessons, outdoor activities and practical field experiences, participants learn about wildlife, ecosystems, and bushcraft skills.","html":"Through interactive lessons, outdoor activities and practical field experiences, participants learn about wildlife, ecosystems, and bushcraft skills."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"The five-phase programme includes monthly meetings and year-end portfolios, with top achievers earning certificates and an accelerated route to becoming full SANParks Honorary Rangers at age 18.","html":"The five-phase programme includes monthly meetings and year-end portfolios, with top achievers earning certificates and an accelerated route to becoming full SANParks Honorary Rangers at age 18."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"During their visit, Dr Anziske Kayster, Khanya Mbaile and Silindokuhle Zito spent nearly three hours sharing insights with the group.","html":"During their visit, Dr Anziske Kayster, Khanya Mbaile and Silindokuhle Zito spent nearly three hours sharing insights with the group."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"Topics included ancient rock art and fossils, life in early Graaff-Reinet, and the town’s role in the Anglo Boer War and the Second World War.","html":"Topics included ancient rock art and fossils, life in early Graaff-Reinet, and the town’s role in the Anglo Boer War and the Second World War."},{"type":"blockquote","text":"The session sparked curiosity and excitement among the young visitors, many of who expressed a deeper appreciation for South Africa’s cultural and natural heritage."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"The Graaff-Reinet Museum extended heartfelt thanks to Rosemary Scheepers of the Karoo National Park for co-ordinating the annual visit, which continues to inspire new generations of conservation-minded leaders.","html":"The Graaff-Reinet Museum extended heartfelt thanks to Rosemary Scheepers of the Karoo National Park for co-ordinating the annual visit, which continues to inspire new generations of conservation-minded leaders."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"‘We bring you the latest Garden Route, Hessequa, Karoo news’","html":"‘We bring you the latest Garden Route, Hessequa, Karoo news’"}]

images.alt: Young rangers explore history at Graaff-Reinet Museum
